name: Black Walnut Bars
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Light brown sugar, lightly packed
2 cups Light brown sugar; lightly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the sugar and butter together in a large bowl until well creamed.
2. Add the flour thoroughly and spread evenly in a greased 9x13-inch pan.
3. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 5 minutes. It will look bubbly with holes all over, but this is fine.
4. Let cool while making the top layer.
5. For the top, cream the brown sugar and eggs together until light and fluffy. Add the vanilla.
6. Sift the salt, flour, and baking powder together in another bowl and add to the egg mixture.
7. Fold in the nuts and pour over the baked bottom layer.
8. Return to oven and bake at 350°F for 20 minutes or until light brown on top and firm to the touch.
9. Cool and cut into bars. Do not cut them too thin or they will crumble.
10. These keep well if stored in an airtight container. Yield: 3-4 dozen 1x2-inch bars.
Note: If you can't find black walnuts, regular walnuts or pecans can be used.
